title: alopecurus pratensis var. exaltatus
intro: Alopecurus pratensis var. exaltatus, also known as tall meadow foxtail, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce bloating, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Alopecurus pratensis var. exaltatus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
